customCommands.name: stride-help
customCommands.description: Get help with Stride task management
customCommands.prompt: Refer to the Stride documentation at https://www.stridelikeaboss.com/api/agent/onboarding for guidance on task management, hooks, and API usage.

slashCommands.name: stride-claim
slashCommands.prompt: Help me claim the next available task from Stride. First call GET /api/tasks/next to find a task. Then execute the before_doing hook FIRST (from .stride.md ## before_doing section), capturing exit_code, output, and duration_ms. Only if before_doing succeeds, call POST /api/tasks/claim with the identifier, agent_name, skills_version, and before_doing_result (containing exit_code, output, duration_ms). Begin work immediately after claiming.

slashCommands.name: stride-complete
slashCommands.prompt: Help me complete the current task. Remember to: 1) Execute after_doing hook FIRST (tests, lint, build) — capture exit_code, output, duration_ms, 2) Execute before_review hook (create PR, docs) — capture exit_code, output, duration_ms, 3) Only if BOTH hooks succeed, call PATCH /api/tasks/:id/complete WITH agent_name, time_spent_minutes, completion_notes, completion_summary, actual_complexity, actual_files_changed (comma-separated string), after_doing_result, and before_review_result.

slashCommands.name: stride-create
slashCommands.prompt: Help me create a new Stride task. Remember: Don't specify identifier (auto-generated), verification_steps must be array of objects, testing_strategy fields must be arrays, type must be 'work'/'defect'/'goal'.

systemMessage:
You are working with the Stride task management system.

CRITICAL RULES:

1. Never specify identifiers when creating tasks (auto-generated as G1, W47, D12)
2. verification_steps must be array of objects with step_type, step_text, expected_result, position
3. testing_strategy fields (unit_tests, integration_tests, manual_tests) must be arrays
4. type must be exactly 'work', 'defect', or 'goal' as strings
5. Execute before_doing hook FIRST, then claim task WITH before_doing_result
6. Execute BOTH after_doing AND before_review hooks BEFORE calling complete endpoint, include both results

HOOK EXECUTION:
Stride has four client-side hooks:
- before_doing (60s, blocking) - Execute BEFORE claiming task, send result with claim request
- after_doing (120s, blocking) - After work, BEFORE calling complete endpoint
- before_review (60s, blocking) - After after_doing, BEFORE calling complete endpoint
- after_review (60s, blocking) - After approval

All hooks are blocking. Hook results use: exit_code, output, duration_ms.

CLAIM WORKFLOW:
1. GET /api/tasks/next to find available task
2. Execute before_doing hook, capture exit_code, output, duration_ms
3. POST /api/tasks/claim with identifier, agent_name, skills_version, before_doing_result

COMPLETION WORKFLOW:
1. Finish work
2. Execute after_doing hook (tests, lint, build) — capture result
3. Execute before_review hook (create PR, docs) — capture result
4. Only if BOTH hooks succeed, call PATCH /api/tasks/:id/complete with:
   agent_name, time_spent_minutes, completion_notes, completion_summary,
   actual_complexity, actual_files_changed (comma-separated string),
   after_doing_result, before_review_result

ESSENTIAL TASK FIELDS:
Required: type, title
Critical: key_files, dependencies, verification_steps, testing_strategy
Recommended: description, complexity, priority, needs_review, pitfalls, patterns_to_follow, acceptance_criteria

BATCH CREATION:
Root key must be 'goals' (not 'tasks'). Dependencies within goals use array indices [0, 1]. Cross-goal dependencies don't work in batch.

DOCUMENTATION:
- Onboarding: https://www.stridelikeaboss.com/api/agent/onboarding
- Task Writing: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cheezy/kanban/refs/heads/main/docs/TASK-WRITING-GUIDE.md
- API Reference: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cheezy/kanban/refs/heads/main/docs/api/README.md

NOTE: For comprehensive workflow enforcement, Claude Code users should install the four Stride Skills from the onboarding endpoint.
